---
title: "4.10 Pairs and Duplicates"
subtitle: "Implementing ArrayList Algorithms"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Pairs and Duplicates

### Consecutive pairs and duplicate elements

In the last lesson, the coding challenge involved finding pairs.

You should be able to write code that:

- accesses all consecutive pairs of elements in an `ArrayList`
- determines the presence or absence of duplicate elements

These problems often require nested loops.

## Mixed-Up Code

### `parsonsprob:: arraylistDuplicatesParsons`

The following method should return `true` if there are any duplicate elements in an `ArrayList` of `Integer` objects.

The blocks have been mixed up and include extra blocks that are not needed.

## Mixed-Up Blocks

### arraylistDuplicatesParsons

::: {.code-scroll .compact style="max-height: 380px;"}
```java
public static boolean hasDups(ArrayList<Integer> a)
{
=====
  for (int i = 0; i < a.size(); i++)
  {
=====
    for (int j = i + 1; j < a.size(); j++)
    {
=====
    for (int j = i; j < a.size() - 1; j++)
    { // extra block
=====
     if (a.get(i).equals(a.get(j)))
     {
=====
     if (a.get(i) == a.get(j))
     { // extra block
=====
     if (a[i] == a[j])
     { // extra block
=====
        return true;
=====
      } // end if
=====
    } // end inner for loop
=====
  } // end outer for loop
=====
  return false;
=====
} // end hasDuplicates method
```
:::

## Mixed-Up Code: Correct Order

### arraylistDuplicatesParsons

```java
public static boolean hasDups(ArrayList<Integer> a)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < a.size(); i++)
  {
    for (int j = i + 1; j < a.size(); j++)
    {
     if (a.get(i).equals(a.get(j)))
     {
        return true;
      } // end if
    } // end inner for loop
  } // end outer for loop
  return false;
} // end hasDuplicates method
```

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: arraylistDuplicates`

Write a method `hasDuplicates` that returns `true` if there are any duplicate elements in an `ArrayList` of `Integer` objects.

## TestDuplicates Starter

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
import java.util.*;

public class TestDuplicates
{
    public static boolean hasDuplicates(ArrayList<Integer> nums)
    {
        // Add code here
        return false;
    }

    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        // instantiate ArrayList and fill with Integers
        ArrayList<Integer> values1 = new ArrayList<Integer>();
        ArrayList<Integer> values2 = new ArrayList<Integer>();

        for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++)
        {
            values1.add(i);
            values2.add(i);
            values2.add(i); // add duplicates to values2
        }
        boolean result1 = hasDuplicates(values1);
        boolean result2 = hasDuplicates(values2);
        System.out.println("Expected Result: false, true");
        System.out.println("Your Result: " + result1 + ", " + result2);
    }
}
```
:::

## TestDuplicates Test Targets

### Output and loop structure

The output from `main` should be:

```text
Expected Result: false, true
Your Result: false, true
```

The Runestone tests also count the number of `for` loops.

Expected loop structure:

```text
2 loop(s)
```

## TestDuplicates Extra Calls

### Runestone method tests

Test 2:

```java
ArrayList<Integer> list = new ArrayList<Integer>();
list.add(2);
list.add(4);
list.add(0);
TestDuplicates.hasDuplicates(list);
```

Expected return value:

```text
false
```

## TestDuplicates Extra Calls

### Duplicate value test

Test 3:

```java
ArrayList<Integer> list = new ArrayList<Integer>();
list.add(2);
list.add(4);
list.add(0);
list.add(4);
TestDuplicates.hasDuplicates(list);
```

Expected return value:

```text
true
```

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

- use a nested loop when checking every pair of positions
- start the inner loop at `i + 1` to avoid comparing an item to itself
- use `a.get(i).equals(a.get(j))` for `Integer` object equality
- return `true` immediately when a duplicate is found
- return `false` only after all needed pairs have been checked

## End

### 4.10 Pairs and Duplicates

Next: Shift, rotate, reverse, and parallel data structures.
